patch.value: Default-safety policy for destructive pm commands (gc keeps delete-by-default; add pm delete --dry-run)

Resolves pm-5k2w L10. Question: pm gc deletes by default (dry_run:false) while normalize/update-many default to dry-run; pm delete had no preview/dry-run. Decision: (1) KEEP pm gc deleting by default — it is an explicit, opt-in maintenance command a user/agent runs deliberately, output already lists removed targets, and --dry-run is available for preview; flipping its default would be a breaking behavior change for scripts and CI. Mitigation: make the help text state plainly that gc removes files by default and document --dry-run prominently. (2) normalize/update-many KEEP dry-run-by-default — they rewrite many items in place, so preview-first is the safe default. (3) ADD `pm delete --dry-run` (additive, non-breaking) so the single-item destructive path has a preview that reports what would be deleted without mutating; pm delete remains immediate without the flag (existing behavior preserved). Rationale: align safety affordances (every destructive command can be previewed) without changing any existing default behavior.
gc help documents delete-by-default + --dry-run; pm delete gains a non-mutating --dry-run that reports the target; no existing default behavior changed; regression tests cover delete --dry-run.

value.text: Evidence: Implemented L10 default-safety preview. pm delete now accepts --dry-run, returns dry_run:true plus target_path and changed_fields:[deleted], and does not remove the item or append delete history. pm gc --help now states gc deletes matched artifacts by default unless --dry-run is used. Verification passed: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/delete-command.spec.ts tests/unit/gc-command.spec.ts tests/integration/help-runtime.spec.ts (46 tests); pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit. Scope note: left shared contracts/completion untouched for coordinator ownership.

patch.value: Default-safety decision implemented: gc delete-by-default remains documented, pm delete --dry-run previews without mutation/history writes, contracts/completions/tests/manual dogfood/full release gate passed.
Implemented default-safety decision: kept gc delete-by-default with clearer help and added non-mutating pm delete --dry-run.
Destructive commands retain existing defaults but have clear preview affordances and docs.
pm delete dry-run previews target without history/item mutation; gc help clarifies default delete; contracts/completions/tests/manual dogfood/full release gate passed.
